prevalence.trend: increasing in urban areas with pollution and lifestyle shifts

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis of pruritic eczema with typical distribution and chronic/relapsing course; exclude scabies, contact dermatitis, and fungal infections

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; topical therapy access is common, but severe disease care is limited

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care and dermatology clinics diagnose clinically; specialist access variable
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Emollients and topical steroids; calcineurin inhibitors when available; phototherapy and systemic agents in tertiary care; biologics limited and often private-sector
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Out-of-pocket costs for emollients and specialist care; limited access to advanced therapies
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Wide availability of topical steroids and growing dermatology services in urban centers
